settings.title: Blooms for the Hospice Ward, Every Friday
settings.excerpt: Our leftover stems now brighten rooms where they are needed most, and it has become our favorite delivery.
settings.body:
At the end of each week we are left with perfectly lovely flowers that are simply past selling. We hated seeing them go to waste, so we found them a better home.

Every Friday we now bundle what remains and drop it at the local hospice, where the staff place small jars in patients' rooms over the weekend.

Past selling, not past their best

A flower stops being shop stock long before it stops being beautiful. Once stems have been in the window a few days we will not sell them as fresh, but they often have a week of life left in them. Rather than let that go in the bin, we sort the best of what remains each Friday, give the stems a fresh cut, and bundle them into small mixed posies that fit a bedside jar. It takes a corner of the afternoon and turns waste into something kind.

The staff tell us the small jars make a real difference to a room, especially over a quiet weekend. That is the whole reason it has stuck.

How the Friday round works

  • We sort the best of the week's leftover stems on Friday afternoon.
  • Each gets a fresh cut and goes into a small jar-sized posy.
  • The hospice staff place them in patients' rooms over the weekend.

It has quietly become the delivery we look forward to most. If you run a ward or a care home nearby and could use the same, come find us at the counter. We would far rather these flowers brighten a room than fill a bin.
